attempted_route: PyTorch U-Net EEG2EEG with ChannelWiseConv1d (groups=channels), np.load sub01/sub03.npy assumed shape (samples,channels,timepoints), MSELoss, Adam lr=1e-3, 30 epochs, batch 64
observation: Crashed in 1s: ValueError broadcasting shapes (17,200,200) vs (1,16540,1) at X_test normalization; actual file layout is (channels,samples,timepoints) not (samples,channels,timepoints)
rationale: The agent hardcoded axis=(0,2) keepdims normalization assuming axis-0 is samples, but the .npy files store data as (channels, samples, timepoints), causing an incompatible broadcast between training mean shape and test array shape.
recommended_alternative: After np.load, call .transpose(1,0,2) to reorder (channels,samples,timepoints) -> (samples,channels,timepoints) before normalization; then compute mean/std on axis=(0,2) as intended. Verify with X_train.shape assertion == (16540,17,200) before proceeding.
